_readme: Copy to config.json and export the named env vars. KEYS ARE NEVER STORED HERE --
this file names the env var to read; it does not hold the secret.
With no keys: everything judge-free (Lanes 0-2) runs on real data. Lane 3 falls back
to the mock provider so the pipeline is testable end to end.
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...        # then set judges[].kind = anthropic
export OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-...        # then set judges[].kind = openrouter
THE JUDGE PANEL MUST BE FAMILY-DISJOINT. Self-preference is causal, scales with judge
capability, and does NOT cancel by averaging -- Claude-3.5 judging its own pairs went
64.3% -> 44.8%, BELOW CHANCE. A panel of three same-family judges is one judge with
extra steps. PoLL: tau 0.778 vs 0.667 for a single GPT-4, at 7-8x LOWER cost --
strict dominance, no tradeoff to weigh.

_notes.temperature: Judges run at 0.0 and the value is part of evaluator_id. A judge bump is a BREAKING CHANGE: the system refuses to rescale old scores (GPT-4 went 84% -> 51.4% on one task in three months).
_notes.rubric_length: Prompt caching needs a >=4096-token prefix to engage AT ALL, silently. Caching alone saves ~$370k/yr at 50M/day -- more than the rest of the platform costs. The rubric must exceed the threshold.
_notes.position_bias: Swap-and-average is ON by default. Earlier advice to skip it relied on <=0.04 from benchmark data; a 541k-judgment study finds >0.10 in PRODUCTION judges. Measure ours before economizing.
_notes.abstention: ~19-20% of items are irreducibly contested. Abstentions route to a human queue and become a free calibration set on exactly the hard cases. Budget for it.
